name: John F. Kelly
summary: Trump's longest-serving Chief of Staff who called him a fascist and detailed his admiration for Adolf Hitler.

keyStatements.quote: Certainly the former president is in the far-right area, he's certainly an authoritarian, admires people who are dictators — he has said that. So he certainly falls into the general definition of fascist, for sure.
He said on numerous occasions that Hitler 'did some good things, too.'

A person that thinks those who defend their country in uniform, or are shot down or seriously wounded in combat, or spend years being tortured as POWs are all 'suckers' because 'there is nothing in it for them.'
keyStatements.context: Statement confirming reports about Trump's remarks on servicemembers

timeline.event: Appointed White House Chief of Staff
timeline.significance: Became Trump's longest-serving Chief of Staff

timeline.event: Called Trump a fascist in interviews reported by AP/PBS
timeline.significance: Unprecedented criticism from former Chief of Staff

credibility.background: Four-star Marine general with 46 years of service, Gold Star father

oppositionReasons: Believes Trump meets the definition of fascist
Concerned about Trump's admiration for dictators
Witnessed Trump's disrespect for military service members
Saw Trump's lack of understanding of history and rule of law
endorsement: Did not endorse any candidate but urged voters to consider Trump unfit

sources.title: CNN Newsroom transcript (Oct. 3, 2023) — John Kelly statement on 'suckers' and 'losers'
Trump said Hitler 'did some good things' and wanted generals like the Nazis, former chief of staff Kelly claims
